title: Walter C Willett | Division of Nutrition at Harvard Medical School
excerpts:
Section Title: ... > Professor of Epidemiology and Nutrition, Harvard T. H. Chan School of Public Health
Content:
Willett has published over 2,000 research papers, primarily on lifestyle risk factors for heart disease and cancer, and has written the textbook, Nutritional Epidemiology, published by Oxford University Press. He also has four books for the general public. Dr. Willett is the most cited nutritional scientist internationally. He is a member of the National Academy of Medicine and the recipient of many national and international awards for his research.

Reference: Association between the Planetary Health Diet Index and growth differentiation factor-15: the Seniors ENRICA-2 cohort
Planetary Health Diet Index Trends and Associations with Dietary Greenhouse Gas Emissions, Disease Biomarkers, Obesity, and Mortality in the United States (2005-2018)
    * Citing Article

American Journal of Clinical Nutrition

· Walter C Willett
... 7,8 Taking into consideration (1) current diets often characterized by unhealthy and unsustainable choices 4,7,8 ; (2) a projected 77% increase in cancer incidence by 2050 due to demographic changes 9 ; and (3) detrimental effects of the food system on planetary health, [4][5][6][10][11][12] it is increasingly relevant to investigate the role of nutrition as a major modifiable risk factor in cancer prevention from a planetary health perspective.

[Health effects of trans-fatty acids: Experimental and observational evidence](https://www.researchgate.net/publication/24410163_Health_effects_of_trans-fatty_acids_Experimental_and_observational_evidence)
Article
Jun 2009
[D Mozaffarian](https://www.researchgate.net/scientific-contributions/D-Mozaffarian-2197528893)
[A Aro](https://www.researchgate.net/scientific-contributions/A-Aro-2221489312)
[Walter C Willett](https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Walter-Willett)
Growing evidence indicates that trans-fatty acids (TFA) adversely affect cardiovascular health. As part of the World Health Organization (WHO) Scientific Update on TFA, we reviewed the evidence for effects of TFA consumption on coronary heart disease (CHD).
We searched Medline publications examining TFA consumption and CHD risk factors or outcomes,...

title: Walter Willett - Wikipedia
excerpts:
**Walter C. Willett** (born June 20, 1945) <sup>[[ 1 ]]()</sup> is an American physician and nutrition researcher. He is the Fredrick John Stare Professor of Epidemiology and Nutrition at the Harvard School of Public Health and was the chair of its department of nutrition from 1991 to 2017. <sup>[[ 6 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 7 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 8 ]]()</sup> He is also a professor of medicine at Harvard Medical School . <sup>[[ 3 ]]()</sup>
Willett is the principal investigator of the second Nurses' Health Study (NHS2 or NHS II), a compilation of studies regarding the health of older women and their risk factors for major chronic diseases. <sup>[[ 9 ]]()</sup> He has published more than 2,000 scientific articles regarding various aspects of diet and disease <sup>[[ 3 ]]()</sup> and is the second most cited author in clinical medicine. <sup>[[ 10 ]]()</sup>
